rows.finalText: The cards answer "What is the next right step in my calling" through Purpose / Calling with a right path emphasis. The sequence begins in Past with Clouds (6), so ambiguity asks for careful pacing and better questions is already in play. Clouds often points to uncertainty around the path, and the need to separate real ambiguity from self-protective confusion before committing further in your calling. Present turns on Anchor (35), bringing long-term commitment and steadiness to the hinge of the reading and asking for one careful adjustment. Anchor colors your calling through the version of vocation that can actually be sustained, not just admired from a distance. Future closes with Tower (19), and the directional message is you may need stronger standards and boundaries when the pivot receives consistent attention. Tower often points to distance, standards, official structure. The path becomes clearest where Anchor (35) meets Tower (19): a blend of stability and institution, asking you to stabilize what matters and release dead weight while staying alert to emotional isolation or rigidity. The spread turns on Anchor (35) more than any other card. Anchor often points to the version of vocation that can actually be sustained, not just admired from a distance in your calling. Anchor (35) with Tower (19) keep emphasizing a blend of stability and institution, asking you to stabilize what matters and release dead weight while staying alert to emotional isolation or rigidity. Stabilize what matters and release dead weight so Tower (19) can land as lived experience rather than just possibility.
